name: Tashelhit
notes: Tashelhit / Shilha (Taclḥit) — Berber (Amazigh) language of southwestern Morocco, the largest Amazigh variety. Written in three scripts: Neo-Tifinagh (the official IRCAM script, an alphabet), the Latin script (Berber Latin alphabet), and historically the Arabic script (a large medieval and modern manuscript tradition). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec has to pick one script and model the emphatic/labialised series and Tashelhit's syllabic consonants; the Standard Moroccan Amazigh (zgh) Tifinagh table is close but is not identical to Tashelhit usage, so it is not asserted here. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in Neo-Tifinagh (Unicode block Tifinagh) or in the Berber Latin orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode tach1250), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode tach1250.
